div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding-top: 16px; margin-left: 2px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 20px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: nowrap;

div.div: Transfer sequence — Drone A wants ROM X (enable_edge = false)

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1128px; height: 1px; padding-top: 92px; margin-left: 52px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: 1. Drone A registers + heartbeats to Overmind (outbound HTTPS). Overmind returns the swarm peer list, each peer with public_ip + local_ip + port. (No persistent connection — this is poll-based.)

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1128px; height: 1px; padding-top: 182px; margin-left: 52px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: 2. A needs ROM X. DownloadManager queues it (one active download) and calls TransportSelector.fetch(request). Tiers are tried best-first with fall-through: [ LAN-direct → direct-public ].

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1128px; height: 1px; padding-top: 269px; margin-left: 52px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: 3. TIER 1 — LanDirectTransport.usable( peer )? Is the peer's public_ip == A's own public_ip (from _build_local_ip_addresses — a local probe, NOT the Edge)?

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 548px; height: 1px; padding-top: 353px; margin-left: 52px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
3a. PEER B — same public_ip (same NAT) ⇒ LAN-direct USABLE.
→ GET https://B.local_ip/peer/roms/… (mTLS, cert-pinned)
→ stream chunks · resume on Range · verify fingerprint/MD5.
✓ DONE — bytes never touched Overmind.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 528px; height: 1px; padding-top: 353px; margin-left: 652px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
3b. PEER C — different public_ip ⇒ LAN-direct NOT usable.
→ fall through to Tier 2.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 528px; height: 1px; padding-top: 533px; margin-left: 652px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 13px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
4. TIER 2 — DirectPublicTransport.fetch (peer C):
→ GET https://198.51.100.20:443/peer/roms/… (mTLS to C's public IP / reachable_url from the probe).

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 528px; height: 1px; padding-top: 653px; margin-left: 652px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
4a. C IS port-forwarded (probe set reachable_url)
→ stream + verify. ✓ DONE (direct WAN).

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 528px; height: 1px; padding-top: 763px; margin-left: 652px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
4b. C is NOT port-forwarded
→ connection refused / timeout. No more tiers (relay + hole-punch need the Edge).
✗ TRANSFER FAILS for this cross-network peer.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding-top: 319px; margin-left: 465px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; background-color: #ffffff;
display: inline-block; font-size: 11px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; background-color: #ffffff; white-space: nowrap;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding-top: 319px; margin-left: 760px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; background-color: #ffffff;
display: inline-block; font-size: 11px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; background-color: #ffffff; white-space: nowrap;
